description: Capabilities document carrying the OPTIONAL limits.max_publish_per_minute field (0.3.0, RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.2; RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.3). The field is the nominal per-agent publish ceiling on POST /contexts, integer >= 1, advisory for producer pacing. A schema-valid document carrying it MUST be accepted; zero, negative, or non-integer values MUST be rejected as schema_violation (the schema pins `type: integer, minimum: 1`). The limits sub-object stays closed (additionalProperties: false) — this is a 0.3.0 additive schema edit, the same posture as registry_receipt on the closed publish response in 0.2.0.

checks: limits.max_publish_per_minute, when present, is an integer >= 1
presence of the field does NOT change RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.3 obligations: per-agent rate limiting is REQUIRED regardless, and 429 responses MUST carry Retry-After
producers SHOULD use the value to pace publishes below the limiter's threshold; Retry-After on an actual 429 remains the authoritative backoff signal
the limits sub-object remains closed: any field beyond max_payload_bytes / max_embedded_bytes / idempotency_key_ttl_seconds / max_publish_per_minute is still a schema_violation (schema-010)

expected.reason: violates minimum: 1 — a registry advertising a ceiling of zero publishes per minute is advertising an unusable endpoint
violates type: integer — fractional ceilings are not expressible; registries round their internal limiter to a whole nominal value

rationale: Round 5 deliberately deferred advertising rate limits to 0.3.0 because the limits sub-object is a closed schema. 0.3.0 adds exactly one optional field for it. The accept case pins that consumers tracking the 0.3.0 schema accept the field; the reject variants pin the value constraints so implementations do not silently coerce a nonsense ceiling into pacing logic. Each reject variant is the accept-case document with the single named value substituted.
